title: Here’s the 7 prospects Bruins added on Day 2 of 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins selected seven players during Rounds 2-7 of the 2026 NHL Draft.
Notable selections included goalies Yuri Ivanov and Roberto Henriquez, and winger Nils Bartholdsson.
Boston traded their 2026 first-round pick to secure winger JJ Peterka from Utah.

title: Native Venezuelan Willson Contreras speaks on devastating earthquakes: ‘I always keep Venezuela in my heart’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Two earthquakes in Venezuela have resulted in at least 1,430 deaths and 68,900 missing, according to the Associated Press.
Red Sox players, including Willson Contreras and Wilyer Abreu, are honoring Venezuela with messages on their hats during games.
David Ortiz is raising funds at Fenway Park to support earthquake relief efforts in Venezuela via the Red Sox Foundation.

title: Jaylen Brown responds to report that he’d be the 7th-best player on an NBA team according to analytics
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticizes analytics that rank him as the seventh-best player on an NBA team.
NBA insider Bobby Marks reported mixed feelings about Brown due to negative analytics views.
Brown has become a prominent trade target despite criticisms from some analytics experts.

title: Red Sox jump on Gerrit Cole early to defeat Yankees in third straight game
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox defeated the New York Yankees 4-1, securing their third consecutive win over the A.L.-leading team.
Red Sox starter Jake Bennett allowed one run on three hits over 6.1 innings; his second career win.
Yankees starter Gerrit Cole gave up four runs in 5.1 innings; raised ERA against Red Sox above six.

title: Bruins trade Fabian Lysell to Colorado for forward Ivan Ivan
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins trade 2021 first-round pick Fabian Lysell to Avalanche for forward Ivan Ivan.
Ivan Ivan played 66 games with AHL's Colorado Eagles, scoring 11 goals and 15 assists last season.
Fabian Lysell appeared in 12 NHL games, scoring one goal and two assists for the Bruins.

title: Forget the celebrities in the crowd, France are the entertainers of the 2026 World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France defeated Norway 4-1 in their World Cup match at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough.
Kylian Mbappe, Ousmane Dembélé, and Michael Olise led France's entertaining offensive display.
France's dynamic attack has made them favorites for the 2026 World Cup, despite Norway's higher xG.

title: Cape DA investigating alleged threats at Sandwich Middle High
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sandwich Middle High student allegedly made a threat against two classmates, prompting a police investigation.
A safety app flagged the threat on a school-issued device, leading to rapid administrative and police action.
The involved student has been removed from school, and the incident is under investigation by local authorities.

title: Man convicted after breaking into the wrong Boston apartment and murdering resident
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Victor Arrington, 40, was convicted of first-degree murder for a 2015 home invasion in Dorchester, Boston.
Arrington mistakenly killed Richard Long after breaking into the wrong apartment during a planned robbery.
Prosecutors used Arrington's iPhone location data as evidence, marking a precedent in digital evidence usage.

title: Some 20 New Englanders are on ‘American Ninja Warrior’ this season. Here’s your in-depth preview.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Season 18 of 'American Ninja Warrior' features around 18 New England competitors, with 12 from Massachusetts.
Taylor Johnson and Addy Herman are notable New England contestants; Herman won the 2024 ANW Women's Championship.
A new three-lane race format, Tripleheader, debuted on June 22, adding intensity to the competitions.

title: Scotland came to Boston. Did romance follow?
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Scottish fans visited Boston for the World Cup, enhancing the city's atmosphere with their presence and charm.
Local restaurant The Haven featured heavily Scottish-themed activities and attracted kilt-wearing fans during the soccer games.
Boston is curious about any romantic connections made between locals and the visiting Scottish tourists during the event.
